name: The Open Sand — The Killing Ground
description: Five hundred yards of flat, featureless beach between the surf line and the shingle, with no cover of any kind — a geometric problem in exposure that the German gunners solved before the first American boot touched it.
atmosphericDetails: The sand is pocked with the small craters of mortar rounds and the larger, wetter depressions where men have gone down and been dragged by the tide.
Smoke from burning landing craft drifts across the beach in low curtains, providing thirty seconds of cover and then clearing to reveal everything.
The MG-42 from WN-71 fires in four-second bursts at precise intervals — the pattern is regular enough to time, but the men who have tried to run between bursts have not all made it.
